title: Droxine

{{Quote|No… it can't be…|Luke Skywalker, on seeing a nightmare demon of Droxine|<ref name="DM" />}}
'''Droxine''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. It was home to the [[carchar]] and [[nightmare demon]] [[Species/Legends|species]], although the latter—[[Sentience/Legends|intelligent]], [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] killers—were believed to have been [[Death/Legends|eradicated]] by the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. However, some survived, and one such [[Demon/Legends|demon]], [[Reist]], was forced to work for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was [[Skirmish on Lapez 3|sent]] to [[Lapez 3]] to kill the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] but was defeated by the [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]].

==Description==
Droxine was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Droxine system/Legends|Droxine system]] and part of the [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]],<ref name="TEAOC" /> located in the north-east of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Outer Rim Extra">{{SWArchive|url=vault/books/atlas/news20090910/index.html|text=''Essential Atlas'' Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}.</ref> The planet had a number of [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]], which were home to the [[carchar]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. These poriferal creatures had a distinctive [[water-vascular system]], similar to that of the [[Aiwha/Legends|aiwha]] of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]].<ref name="GOR" />

==History==
[[File:Reist.jpg|thumb|right|x200px|Reist, a nightmare demon]]
Around [[3643 BBY]], the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] tracked a group of [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]-aligned [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] known as the [[Fist of Justice]] to Droxine, and the resulting [[Skirmish on Droxine|Imperial attack]] ended with the destruction of their base and the capture of the movement's leaders.<ref name="TOR">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Investigation|Fist of Justice}}</ref> Sometime prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[nightmare demon]]s of Droxine were believed to have been [[Death/Legends|eradicated]]. However, some of the demons survived into the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]], when they were used by several of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] specialist [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]]. One demon, [[Reist]], was used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as an assassin. The [[Human replica droid]] [[Altin Wuho]] controlled Reist, and [[Skirmish on Lapez 3|sent]] it to attack [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] on the neighboring planet [[Lapez 3]]; Skywalker, however, attacked and killed the demon with his [[Anakin Skywalker's second lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="DM" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Nightmare demon}}
Droxine was inhabited by nightmare demons, a small, bipedal species covered in fur, except for their twin tails and their head, which resembled a [[Human/Legends|Human]] skull. The nightmare demons were [[Sentience/Legends|intelligent]], [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] killers; the demons caused individuals to hallucinate, seeing a variety of morbid images. When an individual was enthralled by a demon, they were unable to move. The demons were generally thought to have been wiped out prior to the Clone Wars.<ref name="DM" />

Other inhabitants of Droxine worked as [[Transport/Legends|freight haulers]]. Two such freight haulers claimed to have seen the infamous [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] disable a significantly larger [[Starship/Legends|starship]] using a concealed [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] on his [[Firespray-31-class patrol and attack craft/Legends|''Firespray-31''-class craft]] [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back">''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Fans missed Luke battling Reist, 'a telepathic killer from the planet Droxine.' Sounds scary, but Reist was a monkey with a skull for a head.|Jason Fry, in ''Comic Marvels''|<ref name="Comic Marvels">{{InsiderCite|91|Comic Marvels}}</ref>}}
Droxine was created by [[Steve Moore]] and first mentioned in his [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] ''[[the Empire Strikes Back Monthly 149]]: Death Masque'', published in [[1981]]. It next received a mention in the first edition of ''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'', published in [[1989]], although this mention was not repeated in the sourcebook's [[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|second edition]]. The planet received further mentions in ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'', the [[Star Wars Insider 91|''Star Wars Insider'' 91]] article ''[[Comic Marvels]]'', ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', and its [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|online appendix]].
